reviews.review_text:
**Happy Birthday To The Masterpiece of the Manliest Peak Power Fantasy Action Game that revived my childhood from 33 years of gaming.....DOOM 2016**

10 years later and DOOM 2016 still feels like getting powerbombed through the floor of a burning steel cage by pure adrenaline.

This game did not walk so modern shooters could run. This game kicked the front door off its hinges, chugged six gallons of gasoline, punched a demon in the mouth, and reminded the entire FPS genre what “FUN” actually means.

NO RELOAD SIMULATOR.
NO 45 MINUTE CUTSCENES ABOUT SAD DADS.
NO HIDING BEHIND CHEST-HIGH WALLS.

You wake up. A guy starts monologuing. You literally smash the monitor because you have DEMONS TO EVAPORATE.

Mick Gordon’s soundtrack sounds like a forklift doing cocaine in a volcano.
The Super Shotgun should be classified as a human rights violation.
Every glory kill feels like you’re starring in the greatest heavy metal album cover ever painted.

And the movement? SWEET MERCIFUL CHAINSAW WITH BFG JUICE, the movement.
You don’t play DOOM. YOU. ARE. DOOM. You enter a violent spiritual awakening where standing still is illegal and aggression heals your body like some ancient warrior prophecy.

Ten years later this game still runs smoother than butter on a NASCAR engine.
Still looks incredible.
Still plays better than 95% of shooters released since.

DOOM Slayer (you the player) isn’t a protagonist.
He’s (you) a natural disaster with abs.

On this 10th anniversary, I just want to thank id Software for reminding the world that video games are allowed to be LOUD, FAST, STUPID, and ABSOLUTELY GLORIOUS.

RIP AND TEAR. UNTIL IT IS DONE!!!!

Ten years, one sequel, and one prequel after Doom's initial release, all sorts of opinions are forming around which of the three is the best one. What surprises me is that it seems these is very little consensus. I thought Eternal would storm it but a lot of people see 2016 as the better game. There was a small period when I thought the same simply because the guns actually sounded like guns and the game didn't look like a cartoon. There are small and significant reasons why you would prefer one over the other two and I don't find a different opinion something to argue against, especially in these Doom games. I am very happy all three are truly different, and can be appreciated differently by all sorts of players; casual, enthusiast, hardcore, etc.

Now, I did hint at this a bit; to me, now, 2016 is the third best in the current saga. I'm not saying it's bad or anything (I'd never) but when it comes to the philosophy, identity, gameplay, art style, character design, level design, so pretty much anything, I prefer Eternal and The Dark Ages to this one. 
After playing those for hundreds of hours, 2016 feels like Half-Life in comparison, which is insane to say and I can't believe I feel this way. But that is undeniably the strength of the game: the atmosphere, the immersion, coming back to it it's insane how detailed the environments are on a micro-level. It actually has some leftovers of what I'd call a classic, experiential FPS campaign; plenty of detail in usually conventional environments. Back in the seventh gen devs were more concerned about having more variety in their pallet jack models they'd throw into the environments than actual enemy variety. 2016 is not like that, but one could sense some of that design mentality still being present. It also has a lot of the Doom 3 horror vibe as well, which is really interesting. Not something to consider since back at release we didn't have anything to compare it to, really, but playing it on Nightmare/UN and seeing Hell Knights come out of shadows feels very much like its predecessor. Not sure if it was intentional design, or id software just weren't capable of making it any different back then, but it's interesting to notice. This makes replaying 2016 worth it more if I take large gaps between playthroughs, a year or maybe even two.

When looking at the audio-visual presentation, and the aforementioned design philosophy, to me the game looks a bit dated. Not outdated, but now it's much more apparent that the game...plainly said, came from a certain period in time; it was trying a bit too hard to appeal to the cool kids, with the electronica, the glitchy editing etc. I mean, I don't blame them, it definitely worked lol, but after so many playthroughs of its successors, I couldn't help but notice it and see it as a small concession. Hell, the fact that we just call it "2016"...

What didn't age and what I believe will never age is the core gameplay. It's insane how id nailed this one right out of the gate. A crumb stomp on limp and bobbing weapons, ADS, your guy moving at five meters per minute and so many other simply lame and boring FPS tropes. No stone was left unturned; the game would've been received just fine with a conventional arsenal but they went a step further and made guns that do actual damage and which you can carry at all times. And the movement is just a dream. It's fundamentally a game; you press a key and your character just goes. Crazy stuff, I know.

It may seem like I was nothing but negative here but truly I'll only ever have love, respect, and gratitude for Doom 2016. During the seventh gen, and especially at the end, the mainstream was pivoting hard into story-driven, graphics-heavy linear games at the cost of gameplay and replayability. This was on all levels btw, from the development, to critics, and even public demand, as much as I hate to admit it. Doom was the antithesis to all of it. It turned out to be a cosy home for any player who wants a great action game where player agency is key. From the very start it succeeded with flying colours and, what's even more impressive, it has only gotten better. 

Happy 10th birthday, DOOM

Probably the best Doom game. 
The gameplay and gunplay is fluid. 
Mick Gordons soundtrack is full of bangers.
And unlike the two sequels it's still a sci-fi shooter, and not a R-rated saturday morning cartoon like He-Man with dragons and knights.

In my opinion, the best DOOM game ever made. Satisfying gameplay especially with the glory kills, guns are amazing, beautiful visuals that still hold up today and a fantastic OST which is only an understatement. Definitely a must play
Rip and tear! This game is like playing a 10-hour long Mortal Kombat fatality.
Felt short, but that's mainly because of how much fun i had playing this GOATED game fr. Recommended.

I've finished the campaign in around 12 hours, and honestly, as someone who isn't really a fan of FPS games, found it amazing. The game runs really well, has amazing graphics and some of the best gore I think i've ever seen in a game. It still holds up well against the other more recent doom games, and the community online still seems pretty active! 
(I also love how their's pretty much no story its just killing demons in the depths of hell)

Custom Maps (Snapmaps) are amazing, they're really fun and honestly some of them are better than the main game - especially the campaign-like ones.

And, the online mode is also really good, all the modes are great (In team games), and there always seems to be people online to play against.

100% would recommend to anyone who loves FPS games.

Amazing soundtrack, incredible graphics AND optimization, the best fps combat you will ever find and last but not least some of the most well disigned levels i have ever enountered. Got it for 2€ but wouldn't have been disappointed even if it had gone for 20.
this game has made me what i am today. super replayable, kinda simple not like eternal. i will play this game till i die
Doom at its best... unfiltered and unforgiving chaos. Uncompromising demon killing power fantasy. Rip and tear. you will have fun.

[h1] Just kill them all [/h1] 
This game taught other first-person shooters how it should be done. No hiding behind cover, no recovering health. Shoot and destroy, or you will be destroyed.

[h3]  Pros: [/h3] 
Action Speed: The game is very dynamic. The "Glory Kills" system works perfectly – forcing the player into close combat for health and ammo.

[b]Weapons: [/b] They are very powerful. From a typical shotgun to the iconic BFG, each shot makes the player feel powerful. Alongside weapon mods, there are special mini-challenges, such as: Kill two enemies consecutively with a shotgun for 15 times.

[b]Music:[/b] Mick Gordon's soundtrack fits the game perfectly. Industrial metal makes me feel like I am about to blow my head up in the process of getting into a fight. Exactly what I expect and want from this game.

[b]Optimization:[/b] The game looks fantastic and runs well even on weaker machines.

[h3] Cons:[/h3] 
[b]Environments:[/b] Mars environment was cool and unique, but once you get to Hell, all locations start looking the same – mainly consisting of red and brown colors.

[b]Multiplayer: [/b] This part isn't perfect. It looks forced and, of course, cannot compete with single-player game quality-wise.

[b]Boss Fights:[/b] There aren't many of them. As you face them after unlocking all the weaponry, the boss fights tend to be too easy.

[h3] Is it worth recommending? [/h3] 
I would say yes. Despite being based only on killing, the game is extremely fun. If you wish to switch off your brain and have some plain fun, this is what you should choose.

[b] My Rating: 8/10 ⭐ [b]
The Campaign, the Multiplayer and Snapmap. All so good. I wish we got a newer version of the Multiplayer with the later releases.

Bought in 2019, finally got around to finishing it in 2026.
The story matched the vibes of the game very well. The player character (Doom Slayer) has an understandable character despite not saying a single word.

The gameplay is pretty fun. In a way, it feels like a classic doom game in that can (and sometimes have to) search around the map for items, secrets, etc... I liked that you can permanently upgrade yourself and your weapons across levels and it made finding secrets far more rewarding.

For the new standard of boomer shooters, it's pretty alright. A solid 7/10 experience, some of the arena style fights drag on a bit too long, and I feel like there are too many weapons. That said, it is a fine game, good enough even. Some of the better sections had me enjoying the game and once I got the double jump the combat flowed a lot better for me, the game is better when it's faster paced.

so fun. this may be an unpopular opinion, but i prefer this game to the sequel. 
if you want a fast paced action 1st-person shooter, don't hesitate.

I loved playing some very girly/feminine music through my headphones while I obliterate, curb stomp, molest, slash, ripping, tearing, shooting, blasting, punching their jaws off and Nathan those absolute foul demon chuds from hell!

Es un juego extremadamente repetitivo, estresante cuando realmente quieres terminarlo y sobre todo muy sobrevalorado. Para mucha gente es de los mejores shooters, pero no porque lo digan muchos significa que sea verdad.

♥♥♥♥ yeah!!!!
this game is so cool and feels like a game it dose not take its self to seriously and i love that,the world needs more DOOM.

DOOM 2016 was my first entry into the DOOM series. As a reboot of the franchise, the game’s title is simply and bluntly written as DOOM. After the prologue, a powerful music track kicks in—absolutely explosive. The story is also very straightforward: some bad guy opens a portal between Mars and Hell. In the end, our man with the biggest case of morning sickness in the universe fights his way through Hell and closes the portal. The combat feels brutal and incredibly satisfying. When going for 100% achievements, something to keep in mind is that rune collectibles must be completed within the levels themselves, not in the mission select menu outside—otherwise, the achievement won’t unlock. As for the multiplayer achievements, they are absolutely atrocious, requiring over ten hours of grinding to get all of them.

This game is very good, nice soundtracks and the gameplay is absolutely awesome.
the main story is a bit lacking in my opinion and some of the boss fights are very frusterating
i say, buy this game if you like the gameplay less for the story it is not that interesting
great soundtrack,satisfying guns,and nonstop action A modern fps masterpiece demon fear this game 10/10 came for the demons stayed for the carnage doom never gets old

~~ SPOILER ALERT ~~

Most one of the best version from Doom Franchise, the change from basic FPS to Platform based Shooter, the music is top-tier from Mick Gordon and Richard Devine, the actual Horror look of demons especially Cyber Demon or Hell Knight, the voice acting made me deep to the knee into the game.

R.I.P VEGA

I just finished this masterpiece.

This was my first DOOM game, and it definitely made me interested in the rest of the series.

Gameplay: 10/10
Story: 7/10
Graphics: 8/10
Soundtrack: 10/10

The gameplay is incredible, the graphics still hold up well, and the soundtrack is amazing.

The story is simple, but that's not why you play DOOM. You play it for its great gameplay. The Mars-and-Hell setting is a cool concept, but the developers didn't try to make it overly complex. Honestly, I don't think it needed to be, because this is a game you play for its gameplay first and foremost.

Highly recommended.

A classic masterpiece, incredibly well optimized (I´m playing on an office laptop, the furthest thing from a gaming pc and running at a very respectable frame rate on high settings). Definitely worth playing and replaying (currently on my 5th or 6th run).

The game that rebooted the entire DOOM franchise, It still my favorite of all of them. There is something about the whole vibe of the game the others can't match. Of course, the gameplay is a lot slower then the new interations, but It's still a solid choice nonetheless!

Pure, unadulterated adrenaline. The ultimate definition of an action game!

DOOM 2016 is an absolute masterpiece and easily one of the best first-person shooters ever created. Instead of hiding behind cover and waiting for health to regenerate like in most modern shooters, this game forces you to run headfirst into danger. The "push-forward combat" loop is brilliant—if you want health or ammo, you have to aggressively rip and tear your way through hordes of demons.

The gunplay feels phenomenally heavy, fluid, and incredibly fast. Every weapon in the arsenal, from the classic Super Shotgun to the devastating Gauss Cannon, feels punchy and satisfying to use. The introduction of Glory Kills adds an amazing rhythm to the fights, making you feel like an unstoppable force of nature.

And what can I say about Mick Gordon's soundtrack? The heavy industrial metal music dynamically reacts to the action on screen, pumping you up so much that you genuinely feel like the demons are locked in a room with you, and not the other way around. The level design is also fantastic, full of verticality, secrets, and beautiful, gritty sci-fi and hellish environments.

If you want a game that doesn't waste your time with endless cutscenes and just lets you turn off your brain and destroy demons to epic music, DOOM 2016 is perfection.

I love this game! 

One of the extremely rare examples of a reboot done right! DOOM was dead and they brought it back.
Its just a good game. I haven't played Eternal or the new one ...so for now this is my favourite DOOM. I honestly don't know if I can pick a design flaw. Everything about it from level and character design, to the ♥♥♥♥ tonne of secrets to find, to the combat...its just awesome!

So much demon blood!!

If you haven't played this before you really need to! It will show you that good games are possible when investors and share holders and other suits STFU and don't dictate what happens. AND when developers actually listen to what the fans of the game wanted from it!

Классическая мясорубка на пару вечеров. Много разнообразного оружия и улучшений. Противников тоже хватает, при этом их поведение и паттерны атак быстро становятся понятны, поэтому ни обычные враги, ни боссы особых проблем во время прохождения не доставили. Максимум умирал со второй-третьей попытки на боссе и пару раз — из-за слишком большого количества противников на квадратный метр, которые атакуют со всех сторон.

7 тушёных демонических голов из 10.
Great game. Having came from Eternal and TDA i thought this one would feel barebones, but couldnt have been more wrong. Maybe my new favorite

This game, even though there's a newer version of DOOM out, is actually pretty good. I play every now and then and its pretty fun. I would recommend this game to pretty much any type of gamer out there....GL HF.

this game has a good gameplay but the story just meh im a person that doesn't care story but sometime i want a story so i  think this game is good but not to good and not bad to i play this game because i want to know the story but it just kill the demon
A guy just woke up and chose violence, started killing demon. Cool and fun game

very cool game 

you play as the horror
and the demons play survival

Es un gran juego. Muy de su época y corre muy bien en hardware de gamma media en 2026. Lo jugué en la Nintendo Switch y me gustó mucho. Decidí comprarlo para jugarlo en la PC, y si bien el frame rate y la resolución son mejores hay un detalle que me hace no recomendarlo hoy: las putas pantallas de carga. Aún con un SSD M.2, el tiempo para recargar los niveles es estúpidamente largo. Me aburro demasiado. Este juego vale la pena en el modo "Ultraviolencia",  en donde te tocará moriri sí o sí algunas veces. En ese sentido, tener que esperar para volver al nivel o interactuar con el menú es frustrante. No es tan buen juego como para ignorar eso.

This is how you bring back an old legend without making it feel tired. It takes the spirit of classic shooters and turns it into a modern, loud, fast, angry machine where the only real rule is: keep moving.

The combat is the best part. No hiding behind cover, no slow tactical waiting, no overthinking every room. You run, shoot, dodge, glory kill, swap weapons, and somehow the game keeps pushing you harder without ever losing that clean arcade feeling. Every weapon feels good, every enemy has a purpose, and every arena fight turns into controlled chaos in the best way.

The soundtrack deserves its own praise too. The music hits so hard that it basically becomes part of the combat. It makes every fight feel bigger, heavier, and more fun than it probably has any right to be.

It’s brutal, stylish, simple in the right ways, and still one of the best modern FPS campaigns out there. Not just a good reboot, but a reminder of why this series mattered in the first place.

One of the greatest gems you can buy on steam. Revived the boomer shooter an almost extinct form of fps based on move shot and think. The thinking part mostly being centered around the arsenal and enemies.

It is more than a reboot they added new things to the formula like glory kills, weak points, the chainsaw, and double jump. The game is alot more sandbox with how you can defeat enemies than doom eternal but using the best gun, glory killing, and using the chainsaw in a tight spot is still the path of least resistance. It still improve the sandbox though since you now have the ability to get ammo and health when ever you need it. 
 
The gore and visual of this game still look great. Sometimes I prefer the designs in this game like the barons compared to its eternal counter part. Which is really good for a 2016 game. It doesn't run on everything but it is runs so smooth on 2020 hardware. Something about games before covid were so optimized. The models feel larger and they also have alot of detail.
 
Also the Soundtrack is insanely good and unique. It has some of my favorite tracks in the franchise like "if your looking for a fight", "BFG divison", "Harbinger", and "Vega Core"

Great reboot of the DOOM series!

So many weapons and fun missions, defeating monsters with melee attacks feels cool and the game offers lots of cool weapons for you to use! 

The fact that there's a story to follow is a nice touch as there was never really a story in the older doom games! The game offers a good challenge near the end too with tough bosses! 

Great game, i'd rate it a 9/10, definitely worth picking up if you're a fan of FPS shooters!

a good game that is ruined by one issue i know im not the only one experiencing "loading unverified resource",  the common fix is to "verify" your files but this is a temporary fix and most of the time does not work, this error always pops up unless i launch the game in "Dev_mode" which can mess up my game file and also result in me not getting achievements (never cared about multiplayer) im never gonna put a thumbs up unless it gets fixed and i can play the game i paid for properly.
such a clean, amazing game. everything works fine and the demons all have interesting designs.

This is the game that reignited the boomer shooter category of games. It has intense high speed action and loud metal music to get you in the flow. It's really fun and probably one of the best of what it does, and probably the only triple A at that. My only complaint is that it uses combat arenas too much and doesn't use smart level design to deal with demons like other shooters of it's kind. Also the combat to me gives the visual cue of weight and destruction but i'm not really feeling the satisfaction of it in my body (it's just a non issue thing that is probably different for others)
really REALLY fun game. I'd really hope that the next DOOM game isn't woke-ified.

Enjoyability - 8/10
Soundtrack - 10/10
Mod Content - 4/10
Difficulty - 7/10
Story - 6/10

The gameplay is a bit tedious than Doom Eternal but it still is really fun to play.
This game is my favorite instalment in the DOOM franchise. It has arguably the best plotline, weapon balancing, style, and music. My only complaint is that the movement is a bit slow and a bit basic.
It's actually great game but not my type, recommended to those who love fast paced action type games :)

This game has aged like fine wine
This is the best fast paced shooter ever made

 It even mogs Quake and is better than its sequel

Just pure fun and kino from start to finish. While it loses the horror aspect of Doom 3 (which is a shame), the gameplay more than makes up for it.
A worthy successor to the original games.

10 out of 10

I'll forever respect this game for coming out in 2016 and not being woke

2016 was dark year for gaming
Gameplay-wise it is awesome, had some glitches and falling off the map though. Music is awesome.

I don't like FPS. But I think Doom does well as a FPS.

It is brutal, fast-paced and satisfying.

If this is your first FPS, like it was for me, you will die a lot (I chose "Hurt me plenty" difficulty, which is the normal difficulty). I found the game to be brutally hard.

It is intelligent in a sense that there is a lot of weapons, each presenting there advantage and disadvantage against certain types of mobs. You will have to switch between them because of that (and also because ammos are not infinite for each weapon).

You can also create your own map so that player can test them and tbh it is interesting to have a look at them, just to see what other players do.

The game is not too long, not too short (took me like 16 hours, and this is my first FPS. You might finish it in 10 hours if you have some skill). I played 7 more hours for some achievement grinding.

Regarding the artistic direction, it is great. However, the environments are often the same / reused or at least they look like.



However, it is extremely cliché: you are a slayer of demons that serve on Mars because someone opened a portal to hell or something like that. Weapons have name like "BFG 9000" for "Big ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ Gun 9000". Find me something more cliché than that. And it is like this the whole game, especially in the lore (with intergalatical thing, ...) (The lore, by the way, is unuseful). You sometimes get teleported to certain zones without knowing why and then come back to the previous zone like nothing happened. It really is a game where you don't need to think about why you are doing things, you just do them.

Achievements are grindy (collectables, x enemies to slain in a certain way), multiplayer-based (with a dead multiplayer community in certain modes, but still active on others), sometimes both multiplayer and grindy. They sometimes rely on arcade mode or snapmap mode, which is the mode where you create your own map.

If you like FPS, or want to try the genre, this is certainly a must-try. If you are not interested in the genre, you can skip it, the game really is exactly how you think it is.

For me, it was a good experience (not bad, not super cool). I understand why FPS lovers appreciate it. I don't think I will do it, but it even made me want to buy Doom Eternal.

Una experiencia muy piola y repleta de violencia pero un toque corto.

Igual probalo que es una masa.

This game is good fun. As an old fan of the original Doom, this is a great addition and reinvigoration of the series.

This iteration of Doom offers some welcome progression of the Doom storyline since dead pet bunnies to Doom 3's Militaristic horror feel. They do well by moving time forward a good bit; certainly reminiscent of the original Doom's Technological hell-scape environment. The story doesn't slow anything down; meaningful, yet brief. 

The audio in the game really sells the game. The music during fights is awesome. RIP&TEAR! The weapon audio feels heavy and brutal. 

The visuals are a big leap forward from Doom 3. Hat-tip to the artist. The guns and level design are top notch. Nothing feels or sounds cheap. However I do wish the explosions could have been better. I was able to max out the graphics at 1440p (@100%) with my 6700xt. Use Vulkan API over OPENGL.

Final note: Multiplayer works, but is dead. I can't 100% at the moment.

Best game for your eyes and Health,Relaxing 
Joke Aside, after a decade i played this game and its good looking and very testosterone heavy gameplay 
Very fastpase fps game 
Highly recomended if you are a action loving and fps fan 
just go for it

Another gem of the doom franchise, this game brought doom into the modern shooter age with amazing graphics that hold up even a decade later, combat puzzles that will never leave you unchallenged or unsatisfied, and a story that grabs a hold of you and doesn't let go until you've ripped and torn through every last demon in your path. More of a campaign than doom eternal but less of an arcade shooter.
Truly, one of the greatest FPS games ever made. 10 years later, and it has yet to be surpassed by any other Doom title.
beat this on console a while back, the first one i ever beat as i sat down and played it almost all day and its a great game

i really enjoyed my time with this one i dont typically like shooters but i found this one to be quick enough that it didnt bore me and i enjoyed the rotating weapons it all felt very smooth and the upgrades all felt worth while i also enjoyed the minor explorations segmants even if the game was mostly on rails

This game is awesome. Rip and tear through the demons like there is NO tomorrow and have them fear you. I love the game and its art style and always grew up with it, and glad I did. Play this game, you will have no regrets.

After hearing a lot of praise, I decided to give DOOM a try and was pleasantly surprised. I expected to love the soundtrack, but I wasn't expecting the gameplay to hook me as much as it did. Since I enjoy metroidvanias I think the platforming and the many secrets to discover really did it for me.

My only real complaint is that almost half of the achievements are locked behind multiplayer. Even after waiting in queue for 15min I couldn't find a single player so I just gave up on that.

The GRAND F***ING Daddy of fps games.
move over wolfenstein. doomguy is here.
Could not reccomend this game more
runs very well on low end hardware

Ce jeu est tout simplement INCROYABLE, ses graphismes sont parmi les plus beaux que je n'ai jamais vus dans un jeu-vidéo, pas étonnant venant de l'âge d'or des graphismes :P. Son histoire repart parfaitement l'histoire de DOOM, on arrive donc à mêler un Fast FPS super fun avec un lore complexe ? Chapeau ! Vous pouvez être certains que je jouerai à DOOM Eternal quand j'en aurai l'occasion et je suis très content de mon achat. De plus, il ne demande presque rien pour bien tourner, vous pouvez vous permettre de mettre les graphismes en Ultra SAMERE :)
I love the fast paced gameplay. I will say the story is so good and i love the theme of a demon apocalypse.

I liked the game so much so that I wanted to 100% but after seeing the achievements list I just finished the game with minimal achievements. LIke the hell amount of multiplayer achievements like I can't even 50% ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ i spend time online while I couldn't even connect to server yet alone find a player.

doom 2016 is pure adrenaline and honestly one of the best shooters ever. the movement is super fast, the guns feel amazing, and the soundtrack absolutely slaps. instead of hiding behind cover you just sprint around ripping demons apart, which never gets old. it is just non-stop fun from start to finish.

Doom reminded me why I fell in love with video games in the first place. No unnecessary filler, no boring slow moments just pure adrenaline from start to finish. Every fight feels like controlled chaos, and once the music kicks in, you genuinely feel unstoppable.
The movement is fast, the guns hit like trucks, and the combat rewards you for playing aggressively instead of hiding. It’s one of the few games where I never got tired of fighting the same enemies over and over because it’s just that fun.

Also, the soundtrack by Mick Gordon deserves legendary status.

This game doesn’t just reboot DOOM — it resurrects it perfectly. ABSOLUTE MASTERPIECE.

The marriage between heavy industrial metal foundries on Mars in the future with dark fantasy, demons, castles, crypts and tombs present in this game changed me forever. Not as fast-paced or mechanically involved as it's successors, but, to me, this is still one of the most satisfying FPS arena games of all time.

Been about 10 years and graphics still look beautiful. gunplay is phenomenal due to the chunky sounds, powerful kick, and the demons exploding on impact.

Downsides are movement can feel a bit slow and the story tends to drag on but if you enjoy slaying demons that's not such a problem. Experienced some issues with tabbing out even in widows borderless mode however as far as bugs go it was a clean playthrough.

8/10

i do enjoy doom eternal more i think doom 2016 is more of a comeback and the new formats design for doom eternal, i feel as if everything improved in eteral even tho doom 2016 is still an amazing game also happy 10th birthday

DOOM 2016 me trouxe uma sensação que eu não tinha fazia muito tempo jogando videogame: diversão simples e genuína.

Hoje, já na casa dos 30+, pai de 2 filhos, com rotina corrida e pouco tempo sobrando, é difícil encontrar jogo que realmente consiga me prender sem virar quase um segundo trabalho. A maioria tenta ser gigante, complexa demais, cheia de sistemas, árvores, crafting, mapa infinito e mil coisas pra administrar.

DOOM vai totalmente na contramão disso.

Você abre o jogo e simplesmente se diverte. Corre, explode demônio, pega arma e entra naquele loop frenético que lembra exatamente a sensação dos jogos antigos. Dá pra sentir o DNA clássico da franquia o tempo inteiro, mas modernizado da forma certa.

A trilha sonora, a velocidade do combate, o peso das armas… tudo faz você lembrar porque videogame era tão divertido.

Pra mim, foi quase um resgate daquela época em que eu chegava da escola e jogava só pelo prazer de jogar, sem compromisso, sem pressão, sem precisar assistir 4 horas de tutorial no YouTube antes.

Essa versão do DOOM conseguiu me fazer sentir isso de novo. Coisa que poucos jogos modernos conseguem.
This is the most toxic masculinity game i ever played. and it's amazing. im waiting for the Eternal version to be dicounted and i'll be continue my journey.
the game is really good shooter and you have lot of weapons to kill enemies.

In the midst of my second playthrough for it's 10th anniversary, and it still holds up quite well. Graphics are insane, guns are meaty and make you feel like a beast, gameplay is intensely rewarding and gives me hits of dopamine in a way few other games can manage. (also, honestly I just prefer the praetor suit from this game over Eternal's).

Speaking of Doom Eternal, I feel the need to address the elephant in the room. Eternal is a better game, to be honest with you. It does everything this game does and more, and the gameplay is so insanely smooth that I'm convinced ID did not MEAN to make it that good (especially after they only kinda replicated their success in the dark ages, sorry ID I still love that game too).

But this is my review of Doom 2016, not Doom Eternal. SOOOO is it good? absolutely, it's great. It's biggest downfall is being succeeded by one of the best sequels, hell best GAMES, ever made. If you can manage to put that aside and approach this game on it's level without too much comparison, you'll have a great time. Doubly so if you can play this first with no context behind the subsequent games! Highly recommended

Great game in terms of gameplay and map design. Campaign is short and feels a little underwhelming in the ending. Guns are nice to shoot, sound powerful, and have nice upgrades that varies your play patterns. A good game to boot up during the weekday when your boys can't get on. The graphics are impressive even today but again you are yearning for more maps and levels cause the game is so good haha. Solid 8/10 game would recommend.

Pure adrenaline. Havent even finished the game and i can already say that it lives up to its reputation. What i love about it is that the game rewards you for playing aggressively. Lots of weapons and ways with which you can kill those disgusting demons. Oh and the music is the cherry on top of all this. Banger after banger after banger.

Getting this game during its 10th anniversary which costed 2 euros is a steal. Its worth even the full price in my opinion.
A classic game about a classic game. A must play for anyone interested in FPS.

A worthy progress in Doom franchise. New weapons, game mechanics, amazing graphics overall, highly optimized for good performance and lesser lags and hiccups than most games having similar graphics quality. The gameplay is outstanding and the player can discover new things and gameplay methods everytime they play on repeat. Just fabulous, highly recommended !

Still the best DOOM game by a long shot. The most well cooked, true to the original, and holds up like a charm. If anyone is struggling for a choice as to how to enter the DOOM franchise, don't. The answer is 2016.

The story is simple, but honestly, that works in the game’s favor. DOOM knows exactly what it wants to be- a brutal demon-slaying power fantasy with almost no unnecessary interruptions.This game is just pure chaos in the best way possible. It throws you into nonstop action.The movement feels insanely smooth, the guns feel powerful, and every fight feels intense.
amazing game the gunplay the environment the satisfactions of killing enemies and the movement also is top tier

Out of all the FPS games I own, this one is by far the most fun I've had on a campaign mode. It's high-action, so you don't have to sit through long cutscenes. As long as you don't forget which way to go, there won't be two minutes without a fight. Unlike Call of Duty, you are completely alone, so you don't have to constantly follow a leader. Instead, you can explore to find secrets or slightly hidden items that give you tokens to upgrade your gear.

The upgrades aren't just simple damage boosts either—they actually change different mechanics. Some upgrades are found directly on the map, while others are bought with the tokens you earn. It's a bit hard to explain because this is the first Doom game I've played besides the original Doom 1.

Blasting through demons while listening to a good doom playlist goes incredibly hard. The story actually makes sense, too—there aren't really any good guys, but there are plenty of bad ones. this review is likely pointless as most who are interested have already played but this game is genuinely the most fun I've had in awhile with a campaign.

Also, in DOOM, they make the demons pure antagonists. They don't try to humanize them like in the show Devil May Cry (NOT THE GAME, THE GAME IS PEAK TOO). I love this. I will never trust people who try to make demons protagonists, or make some demons good and some bad; this is something I very much dislike about some depictions of demons or hell-dwellers in shows. It's extremely weird to do, especially considering no major religions consider this true—it's subversive. Maybe I'm reading too much into it, but I like that this game doesn't even attempt that.

 ALSO I DONT HEAR MY PC FANS ON FULLY MAXED GRAPHICS AND 1440p SO IT RUNS WELL.

decade old game still didnt dissapoint me i love this game a lot the executions and the gunplay chefs kiss tbh i would play this game again and i loved every part of it, doom is a must series for every power/heavy metal listeners. 10/10. happy 10 years

This Doom 2016 is way better than that new Doom The Dark Ages crap of a game. I was very skeptical with this one considering the latest Doom was an atrocity to mankind, but this one is decent, not so great with the secrets being shown on the map. The best Doom was Doom 64 then Doom 3 BFG Edition those are gems. Considering I got this on sale for like $1 and some change I'd say it's worth it, I just really don't like the fact they make these so called "secrets" so easy and visible on the map. Must have been all the brain rots crying about "the games to hard, the secrets are to hard" making the Devs bend the knee to these bots. I highly recommend you all steer clear from that new Doom The Dark Ages, it's a complete joke to the Doom franchise and way overpriced.
this is the game when u need to release a year of anger in one go. the music placement is on point,  they literally predict the best times to drop that beat, when u destroy the monsters skulls

of all of my games I own on steam as of writing this, DOOM 2016 has to be the best game i have played

I would pay full price just for the soundtrack

runs incredibly smooth even on my laptop, no lag whatsoever, nothing gets in the way of pure death, DOOM and destruction

buy this game, you will be happy

The creation of this game was beautiful in it's simplicity. What did the die-hard Doom fans want? A fast-paced action-shooter where the line between being a god-like killing machine and demon chow comes down to having lightning-fast reflexes, a know-how to every tool and weapon, and the willingness and iron-clad resolve to charge headlong into the forces of hell.

Let me just say, they pulled it off quite masterfully.

DOOM 2016, Digno de la saga DOOM,  buena historia, buen gameplay, calidad y un precio muy accesible. 20/10 simplemente obra maestra, si quieres ver más gore recomiendo el eternal, lo que no tiene de gore este lo tiene en esteroides DOOM Eternal

Back in school, I'd try to play this game and instantly get nauseous for some reason, but the gunplay and movement were so good that I powered through it anyway. Recently bought the game, and it was a much better experience and I loved the journey of 100%ing it.

Runs extremely good at medium level graphics , even on an extremely low end laptop. I highly reccomend this game and it geniunely gets a 10/10 from me. I still have got to play Doom Eternal and TDA , can't wait to do that , but this game is really , really good.
amazing game and Runs great even at ultra. Just remember vulkun

The game that made me love FPS. 
Never been big fun of a genre, the only game I really enjoyed before this was first Call of Duty, but boy oh boy, this one is breathtaking!

Gameplay is bloody groovy, smooth and dynamic, music and voice acting are God-like, visuals are good, level design and enemy variety are interesting, the lore and the atmosphere are awesome. This is not kind of game you play for the story but it's there too - quite simple, but epic and brutal (in a best sense of the word). Being one of those games where gameplay comes first, it's all around complete game where nothing strikes you as lazy or cheap.

Killing demons were never more fun.
This one is so good that I would advice you to try it even if you are not into shooters jazz. 

p.s. That said, above I was speaking of single player campaign, did not care all too much about multiplayer.

almost 10 years later and this game is still perhaps one of the greatest fps games to be ever made

DOOM 2016 manages to do everything right and then some more. while the story is quite decent its the gameplay where DOOM really manages to shine.

Fast paced combat with really not a lot of room for error and the best part is that the difficulty ramps up as you get upgrades. The voice acting is quite good and the entire game is extremely well made

for someone who first played doom eternal i did miss the dash mechanic since it did add a lot to the gameplay but nonetheless the parkour feels fluid and the extremely linear maps still allow for some exploration 


overall 10 years later DOOM is still an absolute must pickup on sale with its beautiful and gory graphics and phenomenal gunplay
holy peak, fun and intense gameplay. would recomend to anyone who likes to run around and kill everything they see. Doom/10
Playing on HDD. The loading time inbetween levels are bit long. But once the game starts, it works really well.

Pros:
The gameplay was fun, fast & aggressive 
Great atmosphere from both Mars & Hell
Mick Gordon legendary soundtrack
Amazing looking demon design 
Solid weapon wheel & upgrades   
Awesome Glory Kills
Well done design on retaining the old-school FPS feel, while being modern  

Cons:
Standard Multiplayer

Great game. Good music, it's fun, nothing bad to say really. Just kinda slow compared to the two sequels.

8.5/10

As my first time getting into Doom this has been a hell of an experience, pun intended. The game is really fun, i like all of the weapons, glory kills are amazing, the atmosphere of the game is fantastic. Can't wait to finish Doom so i can start playing Doom Eternal.
For some reason, my FPS tanked permanently. I was running it at 120fps at 2880x1880 with decently high settings. All of a sudden, though, it tanked to sub-60 no matter what. Even still, good game.
Quite Good, but is last fight is too underwhelming
The first DOOM game I ever played was this masterpiece, I just wished that younger me was more interested in it than I am now

8.5/10. DOOM has a really enjoyable story that doesn't feel like it distracts you from the high-octane gameplay. Instead it supports the gameplay like good set-dressing. level design is great for both combat-arena and exploration purposes. Yes, somehow DOOM also delivers really fun exploring. The game also has a great medium length, which makes it replayable for people who are pretty busy!

and of course... the music is amazing.

Note: I only have experience with the singleplayer aspects of DOOM.

This game is so ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ badass and i personally think it's better than eternal and dark ages. about to start my 7th playthrough. The game is so incredibly well optimized even on Ultra at 5760x1080. Crazy that its 10 years old now though

i have completed my fourth playthrough of this game across multiple platform. doom 2016 is a brilliant revitalization of doom, combining the best parts of classic doom and doom 3 while adjusting for more modern sensibilities and creating its own separate identity that makes it stand out amongst both 3 and eternal. my only negative is that the end of the game becomes a bit boring.
This game kicks ass. Everything about it. Must-play material.
